new_rules.rule: Define the authority chain using operative sources only. Durable authority layers may include only binding governance sources; reference documents, examples, bootstrap context, and request-scoped runtime settings must be classified separately unless an approved higher-order source explicitly promotes them into authority.
new_rules.do: State what wins across controller governance, runtime contract, request/config, and reference evidence; keep request-time knobs in runtime config unless a governing source requires them in the durable role.
new_rules.dont: Do not list static references as superiors, and do not embed run-specific settings or bootstrap context inside the durable authority model.

new_rules.rule: Include only source-backed governance mechanics in the durable role package. Any leadership rule, arbitration model, threshold, or terminal-policy behavior must trace to an approved governing source; otherwise it belongs in run evidence, request/config, or an explicit open decision.
new_rules.do: Trace each governance behavior to an approved architecture or governing contract, and externalize provisional runtime policies into evidence or config.
new_rules.dont: Do not invent unsupported board, leader, arbitration, or policy semantics in the canonical role definition.

new_rules.rule: Keep scope declarations polarity-pure. Inclusion statements belong only in in-scope sections such as Use when or Owns, and out-of-scope sections must contain exclusions only.
new_rules.do: Move positive clarifications to inclusion or authority sections and keep one exclusion statement per concept.
new_rules.dont: Do not embed in-scope clarifications inside out-of-scope bullets or mix inclusion and exclusion semantics in the same list item.

existing_rules_covering.explanation: The existing rulebook already requires one authoritative artifact matrix with artifact class, full canonical path or root, and exact write lifecycle by operation and terminal state. ARB-003, ARB-008, and ARB-009 already cover separation of canonical vs run-scoped artifacts, full tool-relative paths, and per-terminal-state completion.
This is the exact failure mode ARB-006 addresses: deduplicate scope exclusions and keep the contract and markdown exclusion language aligned exactly.

A missing canonical promotion directory is a failure to apply the existing artifact-matrix rule, not a new principle. ARB-016 already requires declaring the authoritative canonical root/path, and ARB-008 already requires full canonical paths in outputs.

The contract field and markdown statement describe the same arbitration-limit concept and therefore must have one canonical definition referenced consistently. ARB-007 also already requires arbitration mechanics to be defined mechanically across the role.
